rationale: Both correctly land on the 'duplicate of #4808' disposition, but the ground truth explicitly points to issue #4809's comment for context, and only X engages with #4809 (framing it as the tracking issue enumerating five call sites, four fixed by #4810, one left for #4808) — Y never mentions #4809 at all. Y also confidently asserts throughout that '#4808... is already merged on main' and fabricates specific verified-looking code-comment text/line numbers, only appending a disclaimer at the very end that merge-state wasn't actually checked, which is a more serious grounding problem than X's approach of flagging its one unverified claim as an explicit open question. X's regression-test-gap observation is also a concrete, verifiable, useful addition; Y's workaround/verification steps are practical but don't offset the fabrication and missing #4809 linkage.
